steps.title: Cover the whole debate
steps.content: The student wants a historical overview of the two theories of vision. That means the sentence should include both extramission and intromission, show that scholars were divided between them for many centuries, and identify Ibn al-Haytham's eleventh-century experiments as the later development that largely settled the debate in favor of intromission. Any option that discusses only one theory or one ancient supporter is too narrow.

steps.title: Avoid partial histories
steps.content: Choice B names the two theories but muddles the history by grouping Euclid, Aristotle, and Ibn al-Haytham together as if their positions were not divided. Choice C mentions Aristotle and Ibn al-Haytham, but it omits extramission, so it is not an overview of both theories. Choice D describes only Aristotle's support for intromission and also omits extramission. Choice A includes the centuries-long division between extramission and intromission and the later resolution through Ibn al-Haytham's experiments supporting intromission. The answer is A.
